label: 9/11 attacks
detail: US demand shock; WTI fell ~$10 in weeks as air travel collapsed and recession fears spiked.

label: Iraq war buildup
detail: UN weapons inspections and US war rhetoric pushed WTI above $30 for the first time since Gulf War.

label: Iraq invasion
detail: Invasion of Iraq began March 20. Oil spiked briefly then fell as quick US victory seemed likely.

label: China demand surge
detail: Chinese industrial growth drove unprecedented demand; WTI crossed $50/bbl for the first time.

label: Hurricane Katrina
detail: Category 5 hurricane shut 25% of US Gulf production; US gasoline hit record $3+/gal nationally.

label: Lebanon War / Iran tensions
detail: Israel-Hezbollah conflict and Iranian nuclear standoff pushed Brent to $78; geopolitical risk premium surged.

label: WTI approaches $100
detail: Combination of weak USD, tight supply, and speculation drove WTI to $99.29 in November.

label: All-time high $147
detail: WTI hit $147.27 on July 11 — the all-time record. Driven by speculation, weak USD, and tight OPEC spare capacity.

label: Financial crisis crash
detail: Global demand collapse after Lehman Brothers. WTI fell from $147 to $32 in 5 months — fastest crash in history.

label: OPEC emergency cuts
detail: OPEC cut production by a record 4.2 million bpd in late 2008. Prices began recovering by Q2 2009.

label: Deepwater Horizon
detail: BP Macondo blowout killed 11, spilled 4.9M barrels. Led to US offshore drilling moratorium and higher risk premiums.

label: Arab Spring
detail: Uprisings across Libya, Egypt, Bahrain disrupted ~1.5Mbpd of Libyan output; Brent spiked above $120.

label: Iran sanctions
detail: US/EU sanctions on Iranian oil exports over nuclear program removed ~1Mbpd from global supply.

label: WTI-WCS blowout
detail: Canadian pipeline bottlenecks caused WCS discount to WTI to widen to $40+. Alberta oil stranded inland.

label: ISIS seizes Mosul
detail: ISIS captured Iraq's second city; Brent rose above $115 briefly before supply fears faded.

label: OPEC refuses to cut
detail: Saudi-led OPEC declined to cut output despite US shale glut. Deliberate strategy to pressure high-cost producers.

label: China stock crash
detail: Shanghai composite fell 30%; commodity demand fears sent Brent below $50 for first time since 2009.

label: WTI hits 13yr low
detail: WTI fell to $26.55 in February 2016 — lowest since 2003. Global supply glut reached ~3Mbpd.

label: OPEC+ Vienna deal
detail: First OPEC+ agreement (including Russia) since 2001. Coordinated cut of 1.8Mbpd took effect Jan 2017.

label: WCS hits record $50 discount
detail: Lack of pipeline capacity caused WCS to crash to under $15/bbl while WTI was $70 — a $50+ discount. Alberta forced output curtailments.

label: Alberta curtailments begin
detail: AB government mandated 325,000 bpd production cuts to drain storage. WCS discount narrowed dramatically.

label: Abqaiq drone attack
detail: Drone strikes on Saudi Aramco's Abqaiq facility knocked out 5.7Mbpd (5% of world supply) overnight. Brent jumped 15% in one day.

label: COVID demand collapse
detail: Global lockdowns cut oil demand by ~30Mbpd. Saudi-Russia price war simultaneously flooded supply. WTI fell from $60 to $20 in 30 days.

label: WTI goes negative: -$37
detail: May WTI futures settled at -$37.63/bbl on April 20 — first negative oil price in history. Storage at Cushing, OK was physically full.

label: OPEC+ record cut
detail: OPEC+ agreed to cut 9.7Mbpd — the largest coordinated production cut ever — to stabilize prices.

label: Ever Given blocks Suez
detail: Container ship blocked Suez Canal for 6 days, disrupting ~10% of global trade including oil tankers.

label: IEA strategic reserve release
detail: IEA coordinated release of 120M barrels of strategic reserves — largest ever — to combat post-COVID price spike.

label: Russia invades Ukraine
detail: Russia's full-scale invasion triggered sweeping Western sanctions on Russian oil. Brent hit $139 in March.

label: Brent hits $139
detail: Highest Brent price since 2008. Russian Urals crude traded at a $30 discount as buyers rejected it.

label: G7 Russian oil price cap
detail: G7 + EU imposed $60/bbl price cap on Russian oil exports, attempting to cut revenues while keeping supply flowing.

label: OPEC+ surprise cut
detail: Saudi Arabia led surprise additional cut of 1.16Mbpd, angering IEA. Described as 'precautionary measure'.

label: Hamas attacks Israel
detail: October 7 Hamas attacks and Israeli military response raised fears of wider Middle East conflict. Brent rose ~5%.

label: Trans Mountain pipeline opens
detail: TMX expansion opened, adding 590,000 bpd of Alberta export capacity to Pacific markets. WCS-WTI spread narrowed significantly.
